behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (log parser/ingestor -> correlation/grouping). MOCK-MISS: this failure appears when fields from different services are handed to grouping logic; a single-function unit test can miss key-shaping bugs (e.g., service-scoped keys). REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: not required. Rejection-gate: System is rejected because behavior text only requires cross-service grouping semantics, not OS/network/subprocess effects.
behaviors.test_description: Feed mixed valid JSON entries from multiple service sources into the real ingestion + correlation modules in-process, then assert one group per trace ID contains entries from all emitting services and no cross-trace contamination.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (timestamp normalization/skew handling -> timeline builder/render model). MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs often occur in handoff between normalization and timeline assembly, not just sort logic. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency; no AI output; no multi-agent chain. Rejection-gate: Unit is rejected because behavior text explicitly spans multiple services and timeline view construction, not only a local comparator.
behaviors.test_description: Construct cross-service entry sets with controlled skew (including edge cases near ±500ms), run through real normalization + timeline assembly modules, and assert final timeline order matches expected causal sequence and boundary behavior at 500ms.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (metrics aggregation/statistics -> anomaly detector -> report formatter). MOCK-MISS: a pure unit test of percentile math can pass while report-flag wiring fails. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection-gate: System is rejected because behavior text is statistical thresholding/reporting, not dependent on real network/OS timing primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Run synthetic latency distributions through real aggregation and anomaly-report modules, including baseline and spike windows where p99 crosses 3x, and assert the anomaly flag appears only when threshold is exceeded.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: module/interface seam (parser errors -> pipeline control flow -> counters/reporting). MOCK-MISS: unit-testing parser exceptions alone misses pipeline survival and counter propagation failures. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required OS/network/subprocess behavior; no AI dependence; no multi-step agentic chain. Rejection-gate: System is rejected because behavior text does not require real file/network ingestion, only pipeline behavior on malformed records.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a mixed stream of valid and malformed log lines through real parser + pipeline stages in-process, assert processing continues, valid entries are preserved, and skipped-count equals malformed-count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: Seam-map: environment seam (runtime scheduling, queueing/backpressure, I/O/subprocess/process performance under burst load). REAL-INFRA: required; in-process mocks typically hide contention and throughput collapse. MOCK-HIDE: mocked clocks/queues/clients can pass while real execution drops entries. Rejection-gate: Integration is rejected because behavior text is explicitly burst-rate and lag under load (10,000/sec), which depends on real runtime/OS behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the built tool/process in a realistic environment and drive a sustained 10k entries/sec burst via a real producer interface, then verify end-to-end counts (ingested==produced), bounded processing lag, and no backlog growth after burst completion.
